---
title: Contributing to {{REPOSITORY_NAME}}
description: Contribution scope, workflow, validation, and community expectations for {{REPOSITORY_NAME}}.
---

## Welcome

Thank you for helping improve {{REPOSITORY_NAME}}. We welcome focused bug
fixes, documentation corrections, lab improvements, and well-scoped features.

## Choose The Right Channel

* Use {{DISCUSSIONS_URL}} for questions, ideas, and substantial proposals.
* Use {{ISSUES_URL}} for reproducible defects and actionable documentation problems.
* Use {{SECURITY_PATH}} for private vulnerability reporting.
* Use {{CONDUCT_PATH}} for private conduct reporting.

Discuss broad changes before implementation so maintainers and contributors can
agree on scope, learner impact, and validation.

## Contribution Workflow

1. Search existing Discussions, Issues, and pull requests.
2. Open the appropriate issue form or Discussion when prior alignment is needed.
3. Fork the repository and create a focused branch.
4. Follow repository instructions and preserve unrelated work.
5. Add or update tests and documentation for behavior changes.
6. Run the repository-specific validation listed below.
7. Open a pull request using the repository template.

## Validation

{{VALIDATION_COMMANDS}}

If a check cannot run, explain why and provide the strongest available evidence.

## Contributor Responsibilities

* Keep changes focused and reviewable.
* Do not submit secrets, credentials, personal data, confidential information,
  or security vulnerability details in public channels.
* Credit sources and comply with applicable licenses.
* Review and validate all submitted content, regardless of the tools used to create it.
* Follow the [Code of Conduct]({{CODE_OF_CONDUCT_PATH}}).

Submitting a contribution does not guarantee acceptance. Maintainers may request
changes, defer work, or decline proposals based on scope, safety, maintenance,
or learning-design considerations.

## License

Unless stated otherwise, contributions are provided under the repository's
[license]({{LICENSE_PATH}}).
